item.title: space shortcode
item.description: The space shortcode provides a mechanism to add some extra vertical space between various elements in a post. note Markdown ignores whitespace between paragraphs Example Let’s say we have a few paragraphs with multiple blank lines in between. Lorem ipsum ... Lorem ipsum ... warning Markdown will ignore those blank lines in between. So the rendered text looks like: Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet consectetur adipiscing elit. Quisque faucibus ex sapien vitae pellentesque sem placerat.

item.title: columns shortcode
item.description: The columns shortcode flows text continuously across multiple columns like a newspaper — different from layout where each cell is independent. Syntax {{< columns [count] [gap] [align] />}} ...text... {{</ /columns >}} Parameters count → number of columns (default: 2) gap → space between columns (default: 2rem) align → text alignment (default: left) vs layout columns → one text block flowing across columns layout → independent content blocks side by side note The examples here are just using text, but the columns shortcode will also allow other shortcodes, images, etc.

item.title: hr shortcode
item.description: Markdown supports basic horizontal rules using ---. However, we can use the hr shortcode to add styled horizontal breaks as an alterative. {{< hr >}} The default is a line using using the mute color which matches the normal text color. Options line → (default) dots dashed fade {{< hr dots >}} {{< hr dashed >}} {{< hr fade >}} Colors mute (default) info note warning success error important {{< hr line info >}} {{< hr fade info >}} {{< hr dashed note >}}

item.title: layout/cell shortcode
item.description: The layout shortcode breaks out of markdown’s single-column flow. Use cell for shortcode/HTML content, md for markdown content. Both can be mixed within the same layout. Syntax {{< layout [cols] [gap] [align] />}} {{</ cell />}}...{{</ /cell />}} {{</ md />}}...{{</ /md />}} {{</ /layout >}} layout params cols → fr values e.g. "1 2" (default: equal) gap → CSS value e.g. "1rem" (default: 0) align → top, center, bottom (default: top) cell / md params text → left, center, right, justify use cell for shortcodes and HTML use md for markdown lists, headings, etc.

item.title: color shortcode
item.description: The color shortcode applies color to inline text without breaking the flow of a sentence — useful when a full hint or flag would be too heavy. Syntax {{< color "type" />}}text{{</ /color >}} Colors match the hint/flag type vocabulary: info → cyan note → purple warning → orange success → green error → red important → yellow Inline Usage Regular text with info colored words inline. Regular text with note colored words inline.

item.title: details shortcode
item.description: Use the details shortcode for optional information that readers can expand. Syntax {{< details [user text] [tint] >}} {{< /details >}} Option (empty) → “Click to expand” user text Tint true false → (default) Basic Example {{< details >}} Without any user params, this just shows ... {{< /details >}} Click to expand Without any user params, this just shows the default “Click to expand” string and whatever is inside with no tinting.

item.title: flag shortcode
item.description: Flags are similar to hints but with different styling; a hint is subtle, where a flag demands attention. Syntax {{< flag [option] [tint] >}} Option info → (default) note warning success error important Tint true false → (default) Examples {{% flag info %}} flags are great for sharing important information. No tint. {{% /flag %}} info flags are great for sharing important information. No tint. {{% flag info true %}} flags are great for sharing important information.

item.title: hint shortcode
item.description: Hints come in several types for different purposes. Without a label, the hint is styled simply. {{< hint >}} This is a simple hint without a label; use for for general callouts {{< /hint >}} This is a simple hint without a label; use for for general callouts The showcase Hugo theme doesn’t support normal markdown blockquotes, as the hint shortcode is essentially its replacement. Syntax {{< hint [option] [tint] >}} Option (empty) → (default) info note warning success error important Tint true false → (default) Examples {{% hint info %}} This is an informational hint.

item.title: img shortcode
item.description: {{< img src="images/rubber-duck.png" width="600" caption="a rubber duck" >}} a rubber duck

item.title: tab/tabgroup shortcode
item.description: The tab/tabgroup shortcodes organize related content into switchable panels — useful for showing the same concept in multiple languages, platforms, or formats. Syntax {{< tabgroup id="optional-id" />}} {{</ tab title="Label" />}} Content here. {{</ /tab />}} {{</ /tabgroup >}} tabgroup id → unique id for multiple groups on one page (optional) tab title → tab button label (default: “Tab”) Code in Tabs {{< tabgroup />}} {{</ tab title="Python" />}} ... {{</ /tab />}} {{</ /tabgroup >}} def hello(): print("Hello, world!

item.title: table shortcode
item.description: Use the table shortcode to add color accents and captions to tables. The color affects only the header bottom border — a subtle accent line between headers and data rows. Syntax {{< table [color] [caption] >}} | Col 1 | Col 2 | |-------|-------| | a | b | {{< /table >}} Color (none) → default info note warning success error important Caption any text string shown above the table optional No Color {{< table >}} Language Type First Release Python Interpreted 1991 JavaScript Interpreted 1995 Go Compiled 2009 Rust Compiled 2010 With Color {{< table note >}} Language Type First Release Python Interpreted 1991 JavaScript Interpreted 1995 Go Compiled 2009 Rust Compiled 2010 {{< table success "Programming languages by release year" >}} Programming languages by release year

item.title: gallery shortcode
item.description: The gallery shortcode creates an image cycler with left/right navigation. Images are driven by natural dimensions — no fixed aspect ratio. Syntax {{< gallery [width] />}} image-path-1.jpg image-path-2.jpg {{</ /gallery >}} Parameters width → max width in px, centered (default: full width) Navigation on-screen ← → arrows keyboard arrow keys while hovering counter shows 1/N Full Width ← 1/5 → Custom Width ← 1/5 → Both galleries operate independently — keyboard input only affects the gallery currently under the mouse cursor.

item.title: shortcode basics
item.description: Hugo shortcodes are reusable content snippets embedded in markdown. Two delimiter styles control how inner content is processed. Delimiters [angle brackets] — raw Inner content passed through as-is. Use when the cell contains: Other shortcodes Raw HTML No markdown needed [percent signs] — markdown Inner content processed as markdown first. Use when the cell contains: Bold, italic text Bullet lists Headings Inline code Examples Shortcode inside a shortcode → use {{< >}} {{< layout cols="1 1" >}} {{< cell >}} {{< img src="photo.

item.title: Starfield
item.description: Move the mouse right to increase or left to decrease speed Starfield is an implementation of Coding Challenge #1 from The Coding Train by Daniel Shiffman. A field of stars flies toward the viewer, simulating warp speed travel through space. Each star is positioned in 3D space and projected onto the 2D canvas, growing larger as it approaches. Moving the mouse left slows the warp, moving right accelerates it.

item.title: ISS Tracker
item.description: ISS Tracker Demo ISS Tracker queries a live API for the current GPS position of the International Space Station, plotting it as a red dot on a map of Earth. As time passes, a red trail marks the path the station has traveled. This demo illustrates two key p5.js concepts: loading external assets via preload() — which ensures the globe image is ready before the sketch runs — and fetching live data from a remote API using loadJSON().

item.title: Bouncing Ball
item.description: Bouncing Ball is the “Hello, World” of p5.js – A ball bouncing within a window.
